title: Harmandir Sahib
description: The Harmandir Sahib, also known as the Golden Temple, is a Gurdwara located in the city of Amritsar, Punjab, India. It is the preeminent spiritual site of Sikhism, famous for its magnificent gold-plated dome and surrounding holy pool (Sarovar).
address: Golden Temple Road, Atta Mandi, Katra Ahluwalia, Amritsar, Punjab 143006
bulletPoints: Holiest Gurdwara and spiritual center of Sikhism
The temple's upper floors are covered with approximately 750 kg of pure gold
The Langar serves free meals to over 100,000 visitors daily
Founded by Guru Ram Das in 1577, the current structure was rebuilt in 1764
The Sarovar surrounding it is believed to have healing properties

title: Jallianwala Bagh
description: Jallianwala Bagh is a historic garden and memorial of national importance in Amritsar, India, preserved in memory of those wounded and killed in the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre that occurred on the festival of Baisakhi, 13 April 1919.
address: Golden Temple Road, Jallan Wala Bagh, Katra Ahluwalia, Amritsar, Punjab 143006
bulletPoints: Site of the 1919 massacre where General Dyer ordered troops to fire on unarmed civilians
Over 1,000 people were killed and more than 1,500 injured
Bullet marks on walls are still preserved and visible
The Martyrs' Well where many jumped to escape bullets
Renovated in 2021 with a new museum, galleries, and sound-and-light show

title: Durgiana Temple
description: The Durgiana Temple, also known as Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Durga Tirath and Sitla Mandir, is a premier Hindu temple situated in the city of Amritsar, Punjab, India. It architecturally resembles the Golden Temple and is dedicated to Goddess Durga.
address: Durgiana Temple Road, Lohgarh, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Often called the 'Silver Temple' due to its ornate silver doors
Architecture inspired by the Golden Temple with a central pool
Dedicated to Goddess Durga and houses idols of Lakshmi and Narayan
Built in 1921 by Guru Harsai Mal Kapoor
Attracts thousands during Navratri and Janmashtami festivals

title: Gobindgarh Fort
description: Gobindgarh Fort is a historic military fort located in the center of the city of Amritsar, Punjab, India. Established in the 18th century, it was renovated by Maharaja Ranjit Singh and has now been opened to the public as a cultural museum.
address: Lohgarh Chowk, Old City, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Originally built by Bhangi Misl in the 18th century, later strengthened by Maharaja Ranjit Singh
Once housed the legendary Koh-i-Noor diamond
Now a heritage museum with live shows, 7D theatres, and cultural exhibitions
Renovated and opened to the public in 2017 after being under military control
Hosts the 'Whispering Walls' sound-and-light show every evening

title: Ram Bagh Garden
description: Ram Bagh, also known as Maharaja Ranjit Singh Garden, is a historical palace garden in Amritsar. It housed the summer palace of Maharaja Ranjit Singh, the founder of the Sikh Empire, and is now a beautifully landscaped public park and museum.
highlights: Summer Palace of Maharaja Ranjit Singh
address: Mall Road, Ram Bagh, Amritsar, Punjab 143001
bulletPoints: Modeled after the famous Shalimar Gardens of Lahore
Houses the summer palace used by Maharaja Ranjit Singh in the early 19th century
Contains a museum with Sikh-era weapons, paintings, and coins
Named 'Ram Bagh' after Guru Ram Das, but commonly known as Company Bagh
The garden spans 84 acres and is a popular spot for morning walks

title: Wagah Border
description: Wagah is a village and border crossing station between Pakistan and India, famous for its daily Wagah border ceremony. Tourists gather here from all over the world to witness the spirited flag-lowering ceremony by both nations' forces.
timings: Ceremony at 5:30 PM (Summer) / 4:30 PM (Winter) — arrive 2 hrs early
address: Wagah, Amritsar District, Punjab 143108 (32 km from city center)
bulletPoints: The Beating Retreat ceremony is held daily at sunset since 1959
Located 32 km from Amritsar city center on the Grand Trunk Road
Thousands of spectators gather daily, creating an electrifying atmosphere
The ceremony involves synchronized marching, high kicks, and flag lowering by both nations
VIP and general seating is available — arrive at least 2 hours early for good seats

title: Akal Takht
description: The Akal Takht, meaning "Throne of the Timeless One," is one of five takhts (seats of power) of the Sikh religion. It is located in the Harmandir Sahib (Golden Temple) complex in Amritsar, serving as the highest seat of temporal authority.
bulletPoints: Established by Guru Hargobind in 1606 as the seat of Sikh temporal authority
The highest temporal seat of authority for Sikhs worldwide
Sri Guru Granth Sahib is ceremonially carried here every night from the Golden Temple
Severely damaged during Operation Blue Star in 1984, later rebuilt
Hukamnamas (religious decrees) issued from here are binding on all Sikhs

title: Mata Lal Devi Temple
description: Mata Lal Devi Temple is a highly unique and popular Hindu shrine in Amritsar. Dedicated to the 20th-century female saint Lal Devi, the temple features a labyrinthine layout, mirrored walls, and cave-like walkways containing various deities.
bulletPoints: Dedicated to the Hindu female saint Lal Devi (1926–1997)
Features a unique cave-like interior with narrow passages and water channels
Contains the famous Sheesh Mahal (Palace of Mirrors) — a fully mirrored room
Devotees wade through knee-deep water as part of the spiritual experience
One of the most unique temples in India due to its maze-like structure

title: Ram Tirth Temple
description: Ram Tirth Temple is an ancient pilgrimage site situated in Amritsar, Punjab, India. The site is the birthplace of Luv and Kush, the sons of Devi Sita and Lord Rama, and contains the ashram of Sage Valmiki, who composed the epic Ramayana here.
address: Ram Tirth Road, Amritsar, Punjab 143105 (11 km from city center)
bulletPoints: Believed to be the ashram where Sage Valmiki composed the Ramayana
Traditional birthplace of Lord Rama's sons, Luv and Kush
A grand annual fair is held here during Dussehra (October/November)
Contains a sacred tank where devotees bathe for spiritual purification
Located about 11 km west of Amritsar city center
